conflict: Nick's creative identity has been colonized by evaluation language; his attempt to paint fails; his withdrawal from Dani is a consequence of the lie he lives inside
consequence: Nick's original self is becoming unrecognizable; his retreat from Dani is not noble restraint but the cost of sustained deception
must_land_beats: Nick's internal language colonized by rubric terms — he can't look at winter light without scoring it
The failed painting attempt: technically proficient, rubric-compliant, dead
Luna's holiday recital — the stumbling performance, the hot gym, Luna's horse drawing as the most alive thing Nick has seen in months
The almost-intimacy with Dani: a kiss in the kitchen, his retreat, his inability to be honest
pressure_source: Nick's creative identity has been colonized by evaluation language; his attempt to paint fails; his withdrawal from Dani is a consequence of the lie he lives inside
secondary_character_beats: Dani as a mother: her pride in Luna's drawing, her patience during the recital, the ordinariness of her domestic life — this is what's at stake in the custody battle
Luna's horse drawing should feel like art in a way Nick's recent attempt at painting doesn't — unselfconscious, proportionally wrong, alive

setups_to_plant.description: Nick pulls away from Dani not from lack of desire but because closeness requires honesty he has made himself incapable of. This withdrawal pattern seeds his inability to confess in Ch 18

state_shift: Nick's original self is becoming unrecognizable; his retreat from Dani is not noble restraint but the cost of sustained deception
